programmes.show_description: Man On The Wall: In the season finale, Chinatown reflects on the aftermath of the riots. Ah Sahm has a new opportunity to go on the offensive. (S2, ep 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following Britain's police. In an eventful shift, Devon and Cornwall cops tackle the region's drug dealers and users. (S1, ep 10)
All in a Days Work: Officers deal with a driver who claims that his glasses case looks like a phone, a motorist causes mayhem on the roads and another refuses his fine politely. (S12, ep 1)
Lead Shoes: A driver blames his shoes for his excessive speed, a night time dog walk turns into chaos and a mechanic gets into a world of trouble. (S12, ep 2)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 5)
New Zealand reality series following the work of customs officers as they prevent drug smuggling, illegal immigrants and more. (S9, ep 6)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S7, ep 9)
Documentary following the men and women who help keep motorists safe on some of the most perilous highways and byways of New Zealand. (S7, ep 10)

Documentary series from New Zealand following the madness and mayhem faced by intrepid officers as they pull over motorists for a variety of suspected offences. (S15, ep 7)

At JFK, officers discover cocaine concealed inside a Caribbean cocktail and a Chinese student hopes to get a pass from CBP after overstaying his visa. (S1, ep 24)
CBP questions a pair of real estate entrepreneurs bound for Florida. In Blaine, a Canadian traveller's US investments are cause for concern. (S1, ep 25)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary series following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists.  (S4, ep 3)

Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 3)
Documentary series following Australia's traffic enforcers. Officers face high-speed chases, defiant hooligans, explosive crashes and out-of-control drunk drivers. (S9, ep 4)
A Canadian crosses the Ambassador Bridge in Detroit by mistake and her bag of medicinal marijuana may cost her. (S1, ep 15)
In New York City, a laptop belonging to a Caribbean traveller does not compute and a young Brit arrives at Detroit Metro Airport to visit an American cyber pal. (S1, ep 16)
A lovesick Lithuanian begs for mercy, customs find enough a truckload of cigarettes, and Biosecurity deal with a sneaky pest trying to hitchhike into the country. (S11, ep 1)
A Nigerian national kicks-off with Border agencies, Customs deal with a fishy parcel from Africa, and Immigration talk to a tricky DJ trying to work without a visa. (S11, ep 2)
Fly-on-the-wall documentary series following the men and women who defend Australia from drug runners, illegal immigrants and terrorists. (S3, ep 12)

Rules Of Acquisition: A Ferengi female, who has defied the law and disguised herself as a male, risks everything when she falls in love with Quark.
Crossroads: An incoming traveller, identified as a Jaffa priestess with whom Teal'C once had a relationship, claims to know how to destroy the Goa'uld. (S4, ep 4)
Workforce: In the first of a two-parter, Chakotay, Kim and Neelix return from an away mission to find the Voyager abandoned, with only the Doctor onboard. (S7, ep 16)
Justice: Captain Picard has a difficult decision to make when Wesley is sentenced to death for innocently violating an alien planet's customs.
Officers suspect a Brazilian man is lying to them and the truth may lay in his bag. A Chinese student in a hurry attracts the attention of Biosecurity officers. (S15, ep 3)
Things go from bad to worse for a young man when drugs are found in his bag and his fiancee is left further unimpressed by what is revealed next. (S15, ep 4)
Two Pink Lines: Bode and his fellow firefighters band together with the civilian station 42 crew to battle a monstrous fire that erupts after a plane crash. (S1, ep 12)
Crestfallen: When a private jet carrying American citizens is shot down over Europe, the Fly Team looks into the man who missed the flight. Season finale. (S1, ep 21)
Broken Bow, Part 2: Enterprise is on its first deep space mission when the Suliban kidnap the Klingon they are transporting, forcing Archer to make a detour.
Exactly the Wrong Time to Get Proud: Feeling the pressure of a beefed-up police presence, the Hop Wei try to survive. The Long Zii gain strong connections. (S3, ep 1 of 10)
Documentary series following one of the UK's largest police forces, Greater Manchester Police, as they tackle crime and battle to keep the streets safe.
Action-packed documentary following Britain's police. From the pursuit of a drink driver to an escalating domestic dispute, cops in west Wales have their work cut out. (S1, ep 12)
Missing Teeth: A marijuana user crashes his motorbike into the back of a car, a learner finds his car is too much to handle and a mishap causes a man to lose his teeth. (S12, ep 3)
Undies. Seatbelt. Simple: A driverless car crashes into a house, a drink driver is pulled over and a speeder accepts his fine with a bit of humour. (S12, ep 4)

At O'Hare International, a returning American's bananas cause a slip-up in secondary. A mother covers for her kid at Detroit's Ambassador Bridge. (S1, ep 26)
Officers discover a traveller is driving without a license and a US resident returns from the Dominican Republic with an unusual package. (S1, ep 27)

In Port Huron, a strong odour leads to a secondary for two Americans and an officer discovers gold at Chicago O'Hare Airport. (S1, ep 17)
CBP officers question a Mexican national with odd travel plans and in Port Huron, a traveller's past may come back to haunt him. (S1, ep 18)
A British national plans his very own Brexit. A package of happiness arrives at the mail centre, and Customs go on a boat-hunt in the Bay of Islands. (S11, ep 3)
An American woman attempts to smuggle a live cat across the border, and Customs find a soft toy stuffed with more than just stuffing... (S11, ep 4)

Necessary Evil: An attack on Quark's life brings Odo face-to-face with a five-year-old unsolved murder - for which Kira was a prime suspect.
Divide and Conquer: SG-1 fear that they may have fallen victim to an untraceable Goa'uld method of mind control. (S4, ep 5)
Workforce: This two parter concludes as Chakotay tries to convince Janeway and co of their past lives on Voyager. Problems arise when he's taken in for interrogation. (S7, ep 17)
The Battle: Captain Picard is accused of firing on an innocent Ferengi vessel in a battle that took place nine years ago.
A woman shocks the team with an incredible revelation as her bags are searched when a drug detector dog raises the alarm. A nervous passenger attracts attention. (S15, ep 5)
The contents of a young woman from China's suitcase could have catastrophic results for an entire industry. Two brothers claiming to be tourists are caught out in a lie. (S15, ep 6)
Follow-Up Day: Each unit is tasked with following up on dormant cases in the hope of discovering new leads. Meanwhile, Nolan gets some life-changing news. (S2, ep 13)
Casualties: National security issues arise as Officers Nolan and Harper investigate a murder. (S2, ep 14)
Fight Or Flight: The crew comes across an abandoned ship of dead aliens who seem to have been the victims of scientific experiments.
